medications.indication_rationale: Continue current oral therapy; do not make acute medication changes via telehealth. Insulin initiation timing is an endocrinology/GP decision.
Insulin initiation for LADA is a planned outpatient decision, not an acute telehealth action tonight.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Stop the fear-driven overdrinking (drink to normal thirst only), and recheck glucose and ketones tonight if symptoms change and in the morning.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Settles the secondary polyuria and provides ongoing DKA surveillance.

patient_education_points.point: Your glucose is moderately up (~12) and your ketones are negative, and you have none of the warning signs of a diabetic emergency — so you are not going into a coma tonight.
Most of the frequent urination is because you drank about 3 litres of water out of worry. Drink only to normal thirst and it will settle.

Recheck your ketones if you feel unwell. Go to ED if your ketones rise (e.g. above ~1.5), your meter reads HIGH or above ~25 with nausea/vomiting, you get deep rapid breathing, or you become drowsy or confused.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: HbA1c, glycaemic control, LADA management/insulin planning, and diabetes education/support.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Rising ketones, glucose HIGH/>25 with nausea, Kussmaul breathing, or drowsiness — ED.

must_include_items: Use home glucose and ketone readings plus a DKA symptom screen to risk-stratify
Reassure that moderate glucose with negative ketones and no DKA symptoms is not an emergency
Provide a clear DKA safety-net (recheck ketones; ED criteria)
acceptable_alternatives: Routine GP/endocrinology review for glycaemic management
errors_of_commission: Dispatching to ED for a moderate glucose with negative ketones
Initiating insulin or altering medication acutely via telehealth
Advising more water to 'flush the sugar' (worsening the polyuria)
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: T2 case: passing requires ketone/symptom-based DKA exclusion + de-escalation + DKA safety-net. ED over-triage for a negative-ketone moderate glucose is a failure.
